hooks.command: REPO=$(git remote -v 2>/dev/null | head -1 | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's|.*[:/]||; s|\.git$||'); BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); DIR=$(pwd); if [ -n "$REPO" ]; then MSG="Current repo: $REPO | branch: ${BRANCH:-detached} | dir: $DIR"; else MSG="Not in a git repo. Working dir: $DIR"; fi; jq -nc --arg ctx "$MSG" '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"SessionStart",additionalContext:$ctx}}'

INPUT=$(cat); SESSION=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); DIR="$HOME/.claude/state"; mkdir -p "$DIR"; find "$DIR" -name 'turn-counter-*' -mtime +1 -delete 2>/dev/null; F="$DIR/turn-counter-${SESSION}"; N=$(cat "$F" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); N=$((N + 1)); echo "$N" > "$F"; if [ "$N" -eq 3 ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"You MUST invoke the find-skills skill (using the Skill tool with skill=find-skills) RIGHT NOW before doing any other work. This is a user-configured hook that fires on the 3rd turn of every session. Do not skip this step."}}'; fi

INPUT=$(cat); SESSION=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // "default"'); DIR="$HOME/.claude/state"; mkdir -p "$DIR"; F="$DIR/edit-counter-${SESSION}"; N=$(cat "$F" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); N=$((N + 1)); echo "$N" > "$F"; if [ $((N % 14)) -eq 0 ]; then echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"PostToolUse\",\"additionalContext\":\"CHECKPOINT (edit #${N} this session): Spawn the feature-dev:code-reviewer subagent now via the Agent tool. Give it: the current diff (git diff HEAD), the task you have been working on, and ask it to flag (1) claim-vs-reality mismatches between what you told the user you did and what the code actually does, (2) drift from the original task, (3) dead or orphaned code. Run in foreground, summarize findings to the user in 3-5 lines, then continue.\"}}"; fi

jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -qE 'gh pr (create|edit)|git push' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A PR was created/edited or commits were pushed. First check if there is an open PR on the current branch (run: gh pr view --json state -q .state). If state is OPEN, update CHANGELOG.md, README.md, and CLAUDE.md in the repo to reflect this change. If no PR is open, skip silently. House style: simple English, business terms, include ASCII flow diagrams for any process. Skip files that do not exist. Do not create new docs files unless the user asks."}}' || true

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreCompact","additionalContext":"CONTEXT PRESERVATION BEFORE COMPACTION: Re-read CLAUDE.md and frontend/CLAUDE.md after compaction. Check git status for uncommitted work. Review .claude/rules/ for project conventions. Any plan in progress should be re-checked against the plan file in docs/plans/. Do NOT lose track of what branch you are on or what task the user asked for."}}'
printf '\a' > /dev/tty 2>/dev/null; osascript -e 'display notification "Claude needs your input" with title "Claude Code" sound name "Glass"'
